verdict_reasoning: BAX is predominantly cytosolic in healthy cells but undergoes conformational activation and membrane insertion upon apoptotic stimulation. It translocates to and inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane (and, importantly, also the plasma membrane) as an integral pore-forming protein. Multiple studies document BAX surface exposure on apoptotic and stressed cells accessible to antibodies from the extracellular face, and anti-BAX antibodies/bispecifics have been explored in oncology. This constitutes a documented, cell-state-induced surface localization via the protein's own membrane-insertion mechanism.
